heading: International measures for seaport and vessel security
label: 70302 International measures for seaport and vessel security
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7030246 App.:1801., , .Pub. L. 99–399, title IX, § 902100 Stat. 889Aug. 27, 1986The word “vessel” is substituted for “shipboard” for consistency in the revised title. The words “and commends him on his efforts to date” are omitted as unnecessary.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7030246 App.:1801., , .Pub. L. 99–399, title IX, § 902100 Stat. 889Aug. 27, 1986The word “vessel” is substituted for “shipboard” for consistency in the revised title. The words “and commends him on his efforts to date” are omitted as unnecessary.

children.text: seaport screening of cargo and baggage similar to that done at airports;
security measures to restrict access to cargo, vessels, and dockside property to authorized personnel only;
licensing or certification of compliance with appropriate security standards; and
other appropriate measures to prevent unlawful acts against passengers and crews on vessels.
